source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A gas turbine cycle has two stages of compression, with an intercooler between the stages. Air enters the first stage at \( 100 \, \mathrm{kPa}, \, 300 \, \mathrm{K} \). The pressure ratio across each compressor stage is 5:1, and each stage has an isentropic efficiency of \( 82\% \). Air exits the intercooler at \( 330 \, \mathrm{K} \). The maximum cycle temperature is \( 1500 \, \mathrm{K} \), and the cycle has a single turbine stage with an isentropic efficiency of \( 86\% \). The cycle also includes a regenerator with an efficiency of \( 80\% \). Calculate the cycle thermal efficiency.

image_dependency: requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate upstream belief that an image is relevant
image_manifest shows 1 available PNG asset and registry_passed=true
question_text itself provides the thermodynamic quantities and asks for cycle efficiency without explicit reference to a diagram, so image dependence appears limited from text alone
multi_step: Question requires combining multiple thermodynamic components: two compressor stages, intercooling, turbine efficiency, and regenerator efficiency
Solving for thermal efficiency requires several sequential state calculations rather than a single lookup or one-step formula
Multiple given efficiencies and temperatures/pressure ratios imply chained calculations across compressor, intercooler, turbine, and regenerator states
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single value: 0.405
choices provide a discrete option set including 0.405, enabling straightforward final-answer checking
registry_passed=true, question_present=true, and answer_present=true support reliable downstream verification

decision_reasons: question, answer, and image assets are all present, and registry passed with no listed asset issues
high multi-step score and high verifiability score indicate strong candidate value and reliable answer checking
risk flags are limited to rounding tolerance and a possible image-dependency conflict, but core semantic assets are intact
